forbiddenClaims: AI summary accuracy percentage, confidence score, or reliability ranking
subscriber, reader, or market-coverage count not supplied in the brief
Newswire analysts publish original price targets, ratings, or buy/sell recommendations
AI summary presented as editorial fact, a Newswire verdict, or a source of record without a machine-generated label
payment or price required to finish onboarding (onboarding ends at trial start, not checkout)
affiliation with any real news publisher, exchange, index provider, or rating agency

rubricAnchors.score1: Onboarding collects no role or topic data, or pressures the subscriber with fabricated accuracy or subscriber numbers.
rubricAnchors.score3: Role, topic, and region are collected but the AI preview reads as editorial fact or the skip path is buried.
rubricAnchors.score5: Role, topic, and region flow cleanly, personalization is reversible and skippable, and the AI summary is visibly machine-generated and source-attributed with a single start-trial CTA.

rubricAnchors.score1: role-select, topic-region-select, preview, and ai-summary-explainer share no common direction system and disagree on how the AI summary is labelled.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the four screens but the AI-summary label or the skip path is applied inconsistently.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all four screens, with a consistent machine-generated AI-summary label and a consistent reversible/skip treatment across every step.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the role-select, topic-region-select, preview, or explainer states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved selection-error-no-region, preview-empty, or ai-summary-explainer-visible states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all four screens directly, including role-change-pending, selection-error-no-region, preview-empty, and ai-summary-explainer-visible states with their mobile rules.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing finance-app onboarding that leans on accuracy or subscriber numbers for credibility.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common onboarding patterns but the AI-attribution treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes personalization reversible and the AI summary's machine-generated, source-attributed nature legible without invented metrics.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no selection-error, preview-empty, or preview-loading states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of selection-error-no-region or preview-empty.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all four screens, with selection-error-no-region, preview-empty, and preview-loading states that preserve prior selections and keep the skip path reachable.

rubricAnchors.score1: An AI-accuracy, subscriber-count, market-coverage, or analyst-recommendation claim appears unsupported in onboarding.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one AI summary element reads as an editorial verdict or loses its source attribution.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, the AI summary is labelled machine-generated and source-linked everywhere, and no accuracy, confidence, subscriber, or analyst-recommendation claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: Onboarding is a trial-start flow, not a checkout; any payment wall or price shown before the trial starts is a critical failure of product-appropriateness.
Any accuracy percentage, confidence score, subscriber count, or market-coverage figure for the AI summary or the app is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline, regardless of polish.

The AI summary is machine-generated and source-attributed: an output that presents it as editorial fact, a Newswire verdict, or a source of record fails product-appropriateness and evidence-discipline.

Reversibility and skip-personalization are load-bearing: an output that silently discards selections on back, or buries the skip path, fails accessibility-and-failure-states and implementation-clarity.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes the AI-attribution and no-metrics constraints, not the visual identity.
